name: TestRail Test Management API
what_it_does: TestRail REST API for test case management platform. Enables AI agents to query test cases, test runs, test results, and milestones. TestRail is the most widely deployed test management tool — AI agents can surface test coverage gaps, analyze historical failure patterns, and integrate QA data into development workflows.
use_cases: Querying test case coverage and test plan status from QA workflow agents
Retrieving test run results and failure patterns from quality analytics agents
Managing test milestones and release readiness from release management agents
Analyzing historical test execution trends from QA intelligence agents
not_for: Test execution (TestRail manages cases, not execution)
Teams without structured test case management needs
Automated functional testing (use Selenium/Playwright for execution)
best_when: A QA team using TestRail wants AI agents to query test coverage, surface gaps, analyze results history, and generate QA reports integrated into CI/CD and project management workflows.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use structured test management — TestRail is overhead if tests run directly from code.

auth.notes: TestRail HTTP Basic Auth with email + API key. API keys generated in user profile. Cloud and self-hosted deployments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Cloud free plan: up to 3 users
pricing.notes: Widely deployed test management tool. Cloud and self-hosted (on-premise) options.

performance.rate_limits: TestRail API: no hard public rate limit; practical limits per plan

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API integration required
Self-hosted TestRail instances have different base URLs — must be configured
Basic Auth with email+key — no modern OAuth; treat credentials carefully
TestRail rate limits undocumented — throttle requests for large test suites
TestRail API returns arrays for most endpoints — pagination uses offset/limit
agent_readiness.security_notes: Test cases may document security-sensitive application behavior. SOC2. Basic Auth credentials must be secured.

security_score_components.security_notes: Test cases may contain sensitive system behavior details. SOC2. Basic Auth with email+key.
